Ecommerce data governance · Founding cohort

Every tool in your stack works. Nothing governs the data between them.

Klints is the neutral governance layer above your marketing automation, commerce, and ERP systems. It scores whether your data can actually carry lifecycle revenue, repairs it through writeback you approve, and hands validated workflow blueprints to the agent you already run.

The silent leak

Your stack looks complete on a logo wall. Your lifecycle revenue says otherwise.

Each system in a mid-size ecommerce stack does its job well. The leak sits between them, in the layer nobody owns.

Marketing automation

KnowsProfiles, behavior, audiences, sending.
Can't seeUnified identity, lifecycle coverage in other channels, acquisition context, ERP economics, inventory and product constraints.

Commerce platform

KnowsOrders, products and checkout: transactional truth.
Can't seeIdentity beyond the order, consent and channel engagement, acquisition pressure, lifecycle stage, active workflows or ERP rules.

ERP

KnowsMargin, cost, inventory, pricing rules.
Can't seeBehavioral intent, consent and engagement, campaign exposure, channel pressure, attribution, lifecycle stage or workflow coverage.

Analytics

KnowsOnsite behavior, acquisition paths, attribution.
Can't seeA governed customer identity, live consent and lifecycle state, workflow eligibility, collisions, inventory context or activation readiness.

Paid media

KnowsSpend, CAC, audience pressure, creative performance.
Can't seeOwned-channel activity, consent, lifecycle stage, active workflows, replenishment and winback timing, or product and inventory context.

Your team

OwnsCampaign cadence, brand voice, tactical execution.
Shouldn't have to ownLive cross-stack mapping, drift detection, identity resolution, collision checks, readiness gates, QA evidence and agent handoffs.
“

Every system is doing its job. What is missing is the architecture between them.

Klints exists to own that neutral layer continuously, across the stack.

The Klints layer

Klints governs. Your platform and its agent execute.

A clear division of labour: Klints supplies cross-stack context, integrity scoring and validated blueprints, then hands execution to the agent inside the platform you already run.

Step 01 · Connect

Read-only connections. A canonical model.

Klints connects to your marketing automation, commerce and, where available, ERP systems through read-only APIs. It unifies what it finds into the klints_ canonical data model. Nothing in your stack changes owner.

Step 02 · Score

The Data Consistency Score.

Deterministic checks plus drift detection across identity, margin, segment, and campaign dimensions produce one hard number: is this stack ready to carry lifecycle revenue, and if not, exactly where does it break.

Step 03 · Repair

Data Fix Blueprints. You approve every write.

For each integrity issue, Klints produces a system-by-system repair plan and executes the fix through writeback that you review and approve. No silent changes, full audit trail, rollback available.

Step 04 · Blueprint & QA

Implementation-ready workflows, validated before launch.

Workflow blueprints cover data requirements, channels, offer economics and measurement. They then pass QA gates that check logic, margin safety and consent before anything ships.

Step 05 · Handoff

Your agent executes. Klints stands behind it.

Validated specs pass the readiness gate and hand off to the agent inside your platform over MCP/A2A, including Manago.ai, where the handoff is confirmed live. The better your agent becomes, the more this layer is worth.

The score

One number that tells you whether your stack can carry lifecycle revenue.

What you get in the product

Six capabilities. One governed loop.

Data Consistency Score

A hard, evidence-based readiness number across identity, margin, segment and campaign dimensions, with drift detection to keep it honest over time.

klints_dcs_score

Data Fix Blueprint

System-by-system repair plans for the activation layer, executed through writeback you approve.

human-approved

Workflow Blueprint Studio

Implementation-ready specs covering data, channel, offer economics, ERP constraints, and measurement.

per use case

QA Validation

Logic, copy, offer economics, consent and measurement are checked before launch. Broken workflows do not pass the gate.

4 gates

Agent Handoff

Structured specs are delivered to the agent in your platform over MCP/A2A, with blueprint plus REST triggers where agents aren't live yet.

MCP/A2A

Opportunity Tracker

Every integrity issue is ranked by severity and projected revenue impact, and every fix is tracked to outcome. Governance shows up on the P&L, not just in an audit document.

ranked by impact

10-question self-check

Is your stack governed well enough to scale lifecycle revenue?

Ten questions based on the problems lifecycle leaders face in production. It takes three minutes, requires no email and provides a design-partner fit signal at the end.

This is an indicative profile, not a diagnosis. The founding cohort starts with the real thing: a full Data Consistency assessment of your connected stack.

Apply for the founding cohort →
The founding cohort

Build the governance layer with us on your real stack, at no cost through MVP1.

A founding cohort of five ecommerce brands works directly with the founding team through MVP1. The deal is deliberately two-sided.

What you get

  • A full Data Consistency assessment of your connected stack. It is the first deliverable, with standalone value whether or not you continue.
  • Full MVP1 access, free through acceptance, expected in Q4 2026, including scoring, fix blueprints, workflow blueprints, QA and agent handoff.
  • Direct roadmap influence. Your friction log sets build priority. Founding partners shape what the category becomes.
  • Founding-partner pricing locked at launch, confirmed in writing before MVP1 acceptance.
  • Founder-level onboarding: you work with the people building the product, not a support queue.

What you commit

  • Sandbox or read-only access to your marketing platform and commerce data, so the assessment runs on reality instead of assumptions.
  • One 30-minute feedback session every two weeks to maintain the friction log that steers the build.
  • Openness to a named case study at launch. It is optional and never a condition of participation.

Cohort size is capped at five so every partner's stack gets founder attention. Fit matters more than speed; we select for ICP match, not first-come.

Now
Apply & connect
Short application, fit call, sandbox connection, Data Consistency assessment.

Founding terms

Questions we expect

The fine print, in plain language.

No. This is structural, not polite positioning. Klints is a division of labour: it supplies cross-stack context, integrity scoring and validated blueprints; your platform and its agent execute. Your current stack keeps doing exactly what it does today. Klints governs the layer above it that no single system can own.

Read-only API connections to your marketing platform, commerce system and, where available, ERP. Writeback is a separate, scoped permission, and every individual write is reviewed and approved by you, with a full audit trail and rollback.

Nothing through MVP1 acceptance, expected Q4 2026. Your investment is access and attention: sandbox connection plus a 30-minute feedback session every two weeks. Founding-partner pricing for after launch is locked in writing before acceptance, so there is no surprise on the other side.

Klints is operated by COMENIUS Agency s.r.o., based in Prague, with EU data processing. Data-processing terms are provided and signed during onboarding, before any connection is made.

No. Klints scores whatever is connected; marketing platform plus commerce is a complete starting point. Connecting ERP later raises the value ceiling, because margin and inventory reality is where the most expensive blind spots usually sit.

Founding partners keep their pricing lock and roadmap influence. The case study is optional. If Klints isn't earning its place in your stack by acceptance, you walk away with a completed data-consistency assessment and repaired activation data. Both are yours.

The thesis
The better the AI in your ecommerce martech stack becomes, the more a neutral data governance layer matters.

Agents execute what the data lets them see. A system of record cannot be the neutral arbiter of its own integrity. Klints is not a system of record, and that is the point. Five founding partners get to build this layer around their own stack.

Apply for the founding cohort → Re-read how it works

The application takes about three minutes. We reply personally within two working days.

Meet the founder

Built from nine years inside ecommerce martech.

Jiří Šefler is the founder of Klints and a martech practitioner with more than nine years of hands-on experience working with SALESmanago, now Manago.ai. His agency, AJ advert, has been a Diamond Partner since 2019.

Across ecommerce implementations, he kept seeing the same structural problem: brands invest in capable platforms, but fragmented data and missing governance prevent their martech stack from working as one system. He created Klints to give ecommerce teams a neutral data governance layer that makes the stack easier to operate while improving the efficiency and return on platforms such as Manago.ai.

AJ advert · Manago.ai Diamond Partner↗ Connect with Jiří on LinkedIn↗ Email Jiří: george@klints.io
